messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] RStudio Server v0.99.489  Ubuntu version 12.04
messages.body_plain:
Hi R-SIG-DEBIAN

We are trying to setup the R Shiny Server using Ubuntu using these<https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download-server/> instructions. Unfortunately when we try to set up the repository to install r-studio into Ubuntu from the terminal the url that we are using does not appear to be right. Here I where we are placing the url /etc/apt/sources.list a and this is the actual URL deb http://cran.rstudio.com/bin/linux/ubuntu trusty/

Can you please advice on the correct way to make the setup, or the correct URL?

Best Regards,

ROBERTO ROMO
Analyst | Market Data Production | MSCI
Parque Corporativo Equus | Ricardo Margain 444, North Tower 9th Floor | San Pedro Garza Garcia, NL 66265 | Mexico

O +52 81 1253 41060
roberto.romo at msci.com<mailto:roberto.romo at msci.com> | msci.com | @MSCI_Inc

Local registered entity<http://www.msci.com/legal/local_registered_entities.html>

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] RStudio Server v0.99.489  Ubuntu version 12.04
messages.body_plain:
On 2 December 2015 at 23:18, Romo, Roberto wrote:
| We are trying to setup the R Shiny Server using Ubuntu using these<https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download-server/> instructions. Unfortunately when we try to set up the repository to install r-studio into Ubuntu from the terminal the url that we are using does not appear to be right. Here I where we are placing the url /etc/apt/sources.list a and this is the actual URL deb http://cran.rstudio.com/bin/linux/ubuntu trusty/
| 
| Can you please advice on the correct way to make the setup, or the correct URL?

I complained a number of times to the dear friends at RStudio that they
should set up an apt-get'able repo. They have not, and you misunderstood the
instructions.

Download the .deb file via the browser, wget, curl, ... or any other mean and
then install that.

Dirk


-- 
http://dirk.eddelbuettel.com | @eddelbuettel | edd at debian.org

I appreciate your help.

Roberto

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] Default R Font Changed After Upgrade to Debian 8
messages.subject_clean: Default R Font Changed After Upgrade to Debian 8
messages.body_plain:
Good day,

The server administrator recently upgraded the operating system from Debian version 7 to version 8 and R from version 3.1.2 to version 3.2.2. The default font used in plots has changed and I've asked him to restore the previous font. What instructions should I tell him about restoring the font ?

--------------------------------------
Dario Strbenac
PhD Student
University of Sydney
Camperdown NSW 2050
Australia
messages.body_snippet:
Good day,

The server administrator recently upgraded the operating system from Debian version 7 to version 8 and R from version 3.1.2 to version 3.2.2. The default font used in plots has changed and

messages.subject: [R-sig-Debian] Default R Font Changed After Upgrade to Debian 8
messages.subject_clean: Default R Font Changed After Upgrade to Debian 8
messages.body_plain:
On 18 December 2015 at 00:00, Dario Strbenac wrote:
| The server administrator recently upgraded the operating system from Debian version 7 to version 8 and R from version 3.1.2 to version 3.2.2. The default font used in plots has changed and I've asked him to restore the previous font. What instructions should I tell him about restoring the font ?

I doubt that this is R-related. R's dependencies have not changed. It
displays via x11 using the resources offered by x11.

So it may have to do with your x11 server setup, maybe via the windowing
environment etc pp.  You may need to compare the list of font packages
between Debian 7 and 8.  You could also try to ask on debian-user.

Dirk

-- 
http://dirk.eddelbuettel.com | @eddelbuettel | edd at debian.org
messages.body_snippet:

| The server administrator recently upgraded the operating system from Debian version 7 to version 8 and R from version 3.1.2 to version 3.2.2. The

messages.body_plain:
Good day,

I have been unable to solve this issue. Do you get grainy fonts when running the test case 

plot(1:10, main = "abcedfghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz", cex.main = 4, cex.lab = 4, cex.axis = 4)

What version of libcairo is on your system ?

--------------------------------------
Dario Strbenac
PhD Student
University of Sydney
Camperdown NSW 2050
Australia

messages.subject_clean: Default R Font Changed After Upgrade to Debian 8
messages.body_plain:
Dear Dario,

if you think this is an issue related to the Debian packaging rather than the 
local configuration, please provide a screenshot of the "grainy" fonts, the 
code you used to produce it, and the output of sessionInfo().

Kind regards, and a good start into the new year to all,

Johannes
